content.boundary: Admission-gatekeeping SES bias is strongly documented, but the effectiveness of any specific proxy, quota, or coaching-resistant exam design in reducing it is only weakly or not yet evidenced in Hungary.
content.change_lever: SES-aware quotas or proxy indicators reserve a share of places for disadvantaged applicants, directly countering the documented ~46% vs ~22% rejection-rate gap between low- and high-poverty-share classes.; A less coaching-sensitive exam format would reduce the weight of family test-prep resources in admission outcomes, though no Hungarian evidence confirms any specific alternative format's effectiveness.
content.name: Admissions and intake-equity regulation (keep structure, fix the gate)
content.system_problem: Reduce family-capital-driven sorting at the point of entry into 6/8-year gimnázium tracks, without dismantling the track structure itself.
